frameworks.name: in-context learning
frameworks.one_line: rule generalisation from sequence context without weight updates

frameworks.name: in-weights learning
frameworks.one_line: memory-based recall of past training examples

frameworks.name: transformer networks as cognitive model
frameworks.one_line: transformers used as benchmark learner to compare with humans

core_question: Do humans and transformer networks learn rules from sequences in the same way under varied training distributions?
key_assumptions: in-context and in-weights learning are dissociable strategies
training-distribution statistics (diversity, redundancy) shape which strategy dominates
transformers are a meaningful comparison class for human sequence learning
key_findings: humans and transformers respond very similarly across training distributions
redundancy drives in-weights learning; diversity drives in-context learning
composite distributions let both strategies operate in tandem
humans benefit from dynamic curricula emphasising diverse examples early; transformers do not
interpretations: shared data-distributional principles govern learning in both humans and transformers
curriculum sensitivity is a human-specific divergence from transformer learning
